channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: FBI accuses North Korean-backed hackers of stealing $1.5 billion in crypto from Dubai-based firm
item.description: The FBI has accused North Korean-linked hackers of conducting one of the largest known thefts of cryptocurrency — worth some $1.5 billion from a Dubai-based firm.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hamas calls for talks on next phase of ceasefire after hostage-prisoner exchange
item.description: Hamas says it was ready to negotiate the the next phase of the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip after handing over the remains of four hostages in exchange for the release of more than 600 Palestinian prisoners held by Israel.
item.dc:creator: Mohammad Jahjouh And Wafaa Shurafa, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Korea appears to have sent more troops to Russia to back its war against Ukraine, Seoul says
item.description: South Korea’s spy agency says North Korea appears to have sent additional troops to Russia, after its soldiers deployed on the Russian-Ukraine fronts suffered heavy casualties.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UK's Starmer will meet with Trump as Europe's leaders worry about drifting US support for Ukraine
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is visiting the White House to try to convince President Donald Trump that peace in Ukraine will endure only if Kyiv and European leaders are at the negotiating table along with Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Greene threatens criminal referrals at House DOGE hearing
item.description: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.), chair of the House Oversight Subcommittee on the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), threatened to make criminal referrals during a hearing on foreign aid on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Golden toilet was stolen from English palace in 'audacious raid,' prosecutor says
item.description: It was not your typical smash-and-grab burglary and the booty was precious: a toilet worth more than its weight in gold.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy expected to sign minerals deal Friday: Trump
item.description: Details are vague but what is known is that the United States is requesting revenue from Ukraine's rare minerals.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Iran can manufacture 6 nuclear weapons with newly enriched uranium, UN Atomic agency report says
item.description: A report by the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) cautioned that the Islamic Republic of Iran is producing a large amount of uranium that could be used in six atomic bombs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says Zelenskyy is coming to the White House to sign US-Ukraine critical minerals deal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Wednesday that Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy will visit the White House on Friday to sign a long-sought minerals deal that will closely tie the two countries together for years to come.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, HANNA ARHIROVA and JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Death toll in Sudan military plane crash rises to 46
item.description: The death toll has risen to at least 46 people, including women and children, following a Sudanese military aircraft crash; at least 10 people were injured.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: High-level EU-US diplomatic talks are called off as transatlantic tensions rise
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A planned meeting between European Union foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas and Secretary of State Marco Rubio was abruptly canceled Wednesday due to “scheduling issues,” coming as political tensions have increased between Europe and the United States.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rubio: Ukraine rare earth minerals deal a 'tush push' away
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Wednesday said President Trump is "very close" to making a deal to gain access to Ukraine's critical minerals as he pushes to end the war with Russia, referencing the Philadelphia Eagle's signature "tush push" strategy.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: More than 100 firefighters battle blazes on South Africa's Table Mountain in Cape Town
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — More than 100 firefighters battled two blazes on the slopes of South Africa's Table Mountain on Wednesday, and national parks authorities said they were starting to bring them under control after the fires threatened to move toward nearby Cape Town.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN food agency pauses aid to famine-hit Sudan displacement camp of half a million people
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The United Nations’ food agency says it has paused aid distribution in Sudan’s famine-hit Zamzam displacement camp of a half-million people as fighting intensifies between the country’s warring sides, and it warns that thousands could now starve.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy looking for ‘NATO or something similar’ as he prepares for meeting with Trump
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy pushes for NATO membership or "something similar" ahead of expected meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Democrat begins CNN interview knocking 'nonsense' Trump-Ukraine report
item.description: Sen. Chris Murphy criticized CNN's Nick Paton Walsh for suggesting that President Trump's intentions on Ukraine are unclear, while Trump's administration has been pushing negotiations directly with Russian officials and excluding Ukrainian leaders.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Nurse who said she wouldn't treat Israelis is charged with making threats
item.description: Sarah Abu Lebdeh, 26, who allegedly said she wouldn't treat Israeli medical patients, was charged with multiple federal offenses in Australia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump envoy Witkoff may travel to Middle East Sunday for Israel-Hamas ceasefire talks
item.description: Steve Witkoff, President Trump’s special envoy for the Middle East, may travel to the region on Sunday if indirect talks between Israel and Hamas on a second phase of a ceasefire deal show progress this week.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement and resumes some work, while still critical, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Monday but showed slight improvement in laboratory tests and resumed some work, the Vatican said, including calling a parish in Gaza City that he has kept in touch with since the war there began.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests showed early kidney failure but he remains alert, responsive and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Afghan women’s radio station will resume broadcasts after Taliban lift suspension
item.description: Radio Begum launched on International Women’s Day in March 2021, five months before the Taliban seized power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel identifies remains of child hostages but says another body from Hamas was not their mother
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — The Israeli military said Friday it had positively identified the remains of two young hostages but another body released by Hamas under a ceasefire deal was not the boys’ mother as the militant group had promised.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, JOSEF FEDERMAN AND WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9
